description_long: This object is a large, rectangular solar energy installation featuring multiple solar panel arrays mounted on a flat, platform-like base. The panels are arranged in several distinct clusters, with some sections elevated on block-like supports and others flush with the surface. The surface is predominantly blue-gray, with black solar panels and a red strip along one edge, possibly indicating a boundary or safety marking. The installation includes various raised structures, likely representing inverters, junction boxes, or other electrical components, and a series of steps or ridges that may serve as cable management or access pathways. The overall design suggests a ground-mounted solar farm or a large rooftop solar array, intended for urban or industrial environments. The materials appear to be primarily metal and glass, with some plastic or composite elements for structural and electrical components.
description: A large, rectangular solar energy installation with multiple black panel arrays on a blue-gray platform, featuring raised blocks and a red boundary strip.
description_view_0: From this view, the solar panel arrays and raised blocks are clearly visible, with the red boundary strip prominent at the front edge.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the varying heights of the panel clusters and the stepped or ridged features along the length of the installation.
description_view_2: The rear view shows the arrangement of the solar panels and raised structures, with the red strip now at the far edge.
description_view_3: This opposite side view emphasizes the linear layout of the panels and the distribution of the raised blocks across the platform.
attribute_panel_orientation: Panels are mostly flat with some slightly tilted at approximately 10-15 degrees; orientation appears fixed.
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with visible block supports, likely aluminum or steel frame.
attribute_array_configuration: Multiple clusters of panels arranged in a linear and grid-like configuration, approximately 4-6 distinct panel groups.
